data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the OWNER of parent SC4 ("Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted, and no probe encodes a bug as expected") and parent AC5 ("Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically; every 'expected-bug' probe guard is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes"). Both FULLY covered:
- 822a AC1 → hermetic bidirectional round-trip of comments/links/parent through the production path (no live Jira) → covers AC5 clause 1 + SC4's "asserted".
- 822a AC2 → every NOT-SYNCED / "as expected" probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes (xfail) or removed; none silently passes on a defect → covers AC5 clause 2 + SC4's "no probe encodes a bug as expected".
- 822a AC3 → hermetic round-trip in CI; live variant opt-in/documented → matches SC4's "real Jira shapes" via the documented live variant.
- 822a AC4 → assertions check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality → applies anti-change-detector posture to its own tests (contributes SC5/AC7).

This child's declared OUT item (producer↔consumer schema/contract → story 8937) is consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 822a itself.

4-bucket summary for this pairing (roster-level): SC4 + AC5 FULLY covered by THIS child; SC1 → 8937+fe57+59ef; SC2 → 33d0; SC3 → 2359; SC5 posture clause across all children incl. 822a AC4; AC1 → 8937; AC2 → 8937+fe57; AC3 → 33d0; AC4 → 2359; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below). No coverage finding attributable to child 822a for its owned criteria.

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already tracked on siblings 33d0/2359/59ef/8937 — restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one (822a AC4), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 822a AC4 "Assertions check round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to THIS story's own round-trip test; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 822a itself for its owned criteria (SC4/AC5 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.
